claimText: Biochemical markers are small molecules, such as sugars, amino acids, and lipids, produced during metabolic processes that are used to ascertain disease risks.
evidenceExcerpt: Biochemical markers are small molecules (sugars, amino acids, lipids) produced during metabolic processes used to ascertain disease risks.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and nature of biochemical markers.
distractorHooks: Large macromolecules like complex proteins or DNA
Molecules introduced externally rather than produced during metabolic processes

claimText: Arabitol, myo-inositol, and ribothymidine are biochemical markers used to identify kidney complications in diabetic patients.
evidenceExcerpt: Components for identifying kidney complications include arabitol, myo-inositol, and ribothymidine.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching specific biochemical markers to their diagnostic application.
distractorHooks: Markers for liver disease or cardiovascular complications
Used for detecting tuberculosis or malaria
